# Database Schema

This is the database schema implementation for the spec detailed in .agent-os/specs/2026-05-04-phase-1-foundation/spec.md

## Azure Table Storage Schema

Azure Table Storage is schema-less. Tables and entities are defined by partition key, row key, and property names written by the application. No migration scripts are required — the SDK creates the table automatically on first write.

### Table: `Thoughts`

| Property | Type | Notes |
|---|---|---|
| `PartitionKey` | `string` | = `UserId` — scopes all queries to a single user |
| `RowKey` | `string` | = `(DateTimeOffset.MaxValue.Ticks - CreatedAt.Ticks).ToString("D20")` — 20-digit zero-padded inverted tick count; produces reverse-chronological sort order within a partition |
| `Id` | `string` | GUID (`Guid.NewGuid().ToString()`) — stable identifier for delete operations |
| `Text` | `string` | Raw thought text entered by the user; max 32KB (Azure Table Storage property limit) |
| `CreatedAt` | `string` | ISO 8601 `DateTimeOffset` stored as string to preserve UTC offset: `DateTimeOffset.UtcNow.ToString("O")` |
| `Timestamp` | `DateTimeOffset` | Auto-managed by Azure Table Storage (last modified time) — do not set manually |
| `ETag` | `ETag` | Auto-managed by Azure Table Storage — used for optimistic concurrency |

### Access Patterns

| Operation | Query Type | Notes |
|---|---|---|
| Create thought | Single entity insert | PartitionKey = UserId, RowKey = inverted ticks |
| List thoughts (paginated) | Partition scan with continuation token | Filter: `PartitionKey eq '{userId}'`; page size up to 100 |
| Delete thought by Id | Partition scan by Id property, then delete by PK+RK | First query by Id to resolve RowKey, then point delete |
| Get thought by Id | Partition scan by Id property | Filter: `PartitionKey eq '{userId}' and Id eq '{id}'` |

### InMemory Equivalent

`InMemoryThoughtRepository` mirrors the same data structure using `ConcurrentDictionary<string, List<ThoughtEntry>>` keyed by `UserId`. Entries are sorted by `CreatedAt` descending in memory.

### Table Naming Convention

- Production: `Thoughts`
- The table name is configurable via `appsettings.json` under `AzureTableStorage:ThoughtsTableName` with default value `"Thoughts"`
